commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `sweepStaleBackupArtifacts()` helper that scans `os.tmpdir()` for old `openclaw-backup-*` directories and `*.<uuid>.tmp` files before `createBackupArchive()` starts a real backup.

PR surface: Source +54, Tests +76. Total +130 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current `createBackupArchive` creates a temp archive and staging directory and removes both only in normal in-process cleanup. I did not run a destructive hard-kill reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor real-behavior proof plus maintainer choice on whether to repair this conflicting branch or replace it from the canonical issue.

target.title: fix(backup): sweep stale temp artifacts on startup before creating ne…
target.body:
…w backup

On hard kill (SIGKILL, OOM, host reboot), the in-process finally block in createBackupArchive cannot fire, orphaning multi-GB staging dirs (openclaw-backup-*) and temp archives (*.uuid.tmp) in os.tmpdir(). Nothing reaps them, so they accumulate across interrupted runs.

Add sweepStaleBackupArtifacts() that runs at the start of each backup create, removing the tool's own stale artifacts older than a 1-hour grace window. This follows the same pattern as #92891 (memory backend reindex temps).

Fixes #95582
